proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous variable feature_078 (range 30-90, mean ~65, plausibly age) is positively associated with pfs_months in this prostate cancer cohort: higher feature_078 corresponds to longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.pearsonr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = 0.8555 between feature_078 and pfs_months (p ~ 0). Linear regression slope = 0.174 months of pfs per unit of feature_078. Direction positive, magnitude very large; relationship is essentially deterministic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_051 == 1 (binary, ~55% prevalence) have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051 == 0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.509 (feature_051=1) vs 4.031 (feature_051=0); difference = -0.521 months (Welch t-test p = 1.58e-181). Direction confirms hypothesis: feature_051==1 is associated with shorter pfs_months in unadjusted analysis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 3-level ordinal feature_057 (values 0/1/2 with prevalences 35%/50%/15%, plausibly performance status or stage tier) is monotonically negatively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_057 → shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_057 level: 0 -> 4.681, 1 -> 3.492, 2 -> 2.376. ANOVA F = 4445.9, p ~ 0. Spearman rho = -0.386 (p ~ 0). Each step up in feature_057 corresponds to ~1.15 month decrement in pfs_months; strong monotonic negative gradient confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (continuous, range 0.12-3622, heavily right-skewed, plausibly a serum biomarker such as PSA) is negatively associated with pfs_months on the log scale: higher log(feature_013) → shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r between log(feature_013) and pfs_months = -0.231 (p ~ 0). Raw-scale r = -0.153. Log transform is the cleaner association, confirming the hypothesized negative log-linear relationship.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (continuous, range 0-24.6, mean 3.84) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_009 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.80, plausibly serum albumin) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher values → longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r between feature_006 and pfs_months = -0.143 (p = 9.9e-226). Negative direction confirmed.
Pearson r between feature_009 and pfs_months = +0.123 (p = 1.5e-166). Positive direction confirmed; magnitude consistent with a labs-scale prognostic variable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_018: white/black/hispanic/asian/other) is associated with differential pfs_months at the population level, with non-white patients having shorter pfs than white patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: asian 3.797, black 3.741, hispanic 3.737, other 3.749, white 3.741. ANOVA F = 0.564, p = 0.689. No detectable racial difference in marginal pfs_months. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance type (feature_045: medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured) is associated with differential pfs_months: uninsured and medicaid patients have shorter pfs than private/medicare patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: medicare 3.754, medicaid 3.741, private 3.736, uninsured 3.726. ANOVA F = 0.344, p = 0.794. No detectable difference by insurance category. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The unadjusted -0.52 month association of feature_051 with pfs_months (h2) reflects confounding by feature_013 (a probable disease-burden biomarker), and after adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, log(feature_013), feature_006, and feature_009 the effect of feature_051 is attenuated to near zero.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_feature_013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_051 = -0.0018 (p = 0.641, NS). The univariate -0.52 is fully explained by feature_051's strong association with high log(feature_013) (point-biserial r = +0.558; mean log(feature_013) is 1.99 in feature_051==0 vs 3.50 in ==1). Refined hypothesis supported: feature_051 is a confounded marker, not an independent predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 (binary, prevalence 10%) is independently positively associated with pfs_months after adjustment for the major prognostic factors (feature_078, feature_057, log_feature_013, feature_006, feature_009).
feature_039 (binary, prevalence 10%) is independently positively associated with pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109 + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_feature_013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_109 = +0.152 (p = 5.9e-181). Positive independent association confirmed; ~0.15 month longer pfs_months in feature_109==1 patients holding other prognostics fixed.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_039 + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_feature_013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_039 = +0.149 (p = 6.7e-177). Positive independent association confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_105 (binary, prevalence 6%) shows borderline univariate association (p=0.015), but its effect on pfs_months disappears after adjusting for the major prognostic factors.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_105 + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_feature_013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_105 = +0.003 (p = 0.647, NS). The marginal univariate signal is fully attenuated by adjustment, consistent with confounding by the multivariate prognostic block. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, log_feature_013, feature_006, and feature_009, race (feature_018) still shows no association with pfs_months — i.e., the lack of marginal racial differences is not driven by counterbalancing prognostic factor distributions.

analyses.code: anova_lm(model_no_race, model_with_race) where both adjust for feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_feature_013 + feature_006 + feature_009
analyses.result_summary: Partial F-test for adding race: F(4, 49989) = 0.097, p = 0.983. No detectable adjusted racial difference in pfs_months. Hypothesis supported: race is null both marginally and conditionally.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the major prognostic factors, insurance (feature_045) shows no association with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Partial F-test for adding insurance: F(3, 49990) = 0.013, p = 0.998. No detectable adjusted insurance-related difference in pfs_months. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is modified by race (feature_018): the (already-confounded) feature_051 signal differs across racial groups.

analyses.result_summary: Partial F-test for the 4-df feature_051 x race interaction: F = 1.38, p = 0.238. No detectable effect modification by race. Hypothesis refuted; the small unadjusted by-race differences in raw means (e.g., asian -0.535, hispanic -0.529, white -0.525, black -0.506, other -0.366 month gaps) are not statistically distinguishable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is modified by insurance (feature_045): uninsured/medicaid patients show a different feature_051 association than private/medicare patients.

analyses.result_summary: Partial F-test for the 3-df feature_051 x insurance interaction: F = 0.85, p = 0.468. No detectable effect modification by insurance. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is modified by feature_057 (the 3-level prognostic stratifier): the residual feature_051 effect varies in magnitude across feature_057 levels.

analyses.result_summary: Partial F-test for the 2-df feature_051 x feature_057 interaction: F = 18.88, p = 6.4e-9. Statistically significant. Interaction estimates: feature_051 main effect at feature_057=0 is -0.019 months; the interaction terms add +0.016 at level 1 and +0.061 at level 2, indicating that any residual feature_051 deficit attenuates as feature_057 worsens. Magnitudes are tiny (~0.06 month) but the interaction is robust given n=50000.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_078 (likely age) on pfs_months is modified by feature_057: the per-unit feature_078 slope is steepest in feature_057==0 (best prognosis tier) and is attenuated as feature_057 worsens.

analyses.result_summary: Partial F-test for feature_078 x feature_057 interaction: F = 983.6, p ~ 0. Slopes: at feature_057=0, +0.180 months/unit; at feature_057=1, +0.180 - 0.006 = +0.174; at feature_057=2, +0.180 - 0.021 = +0.159. The age-PFS gradient is reduced in worse-prognosis strata. Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of log(feature_013) on pfs_months is modified by feature_057: the negative slope of log(feature_013) becomes more pronounced in worse-prognosis (higher feature_057) strata.

analyses.result_summary: Partial F-test for log(feature_013) x feature_057 interaction: F = 80.2, p = 1.7e-35. Significant heterogeneity: the relationship between the biomarker and outcome is not uniform across feature_057 strata. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_006 on pfs_months is modified by feature_057.

analyses.result_summary: Partial F-test: F = 23.6, p = 5.5e-11. Significant interaction: the slope of feature_006 vs pfs_months differs across feature_057 levels. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_078 (age) on pfs_months differs across racial groups (feature_078 x race interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Partial F-test for feature_078 x race interaction: F(4, 49985) = 1.72, p = 0.142. No detectable racial heterogeneity in the age-PFS gradient. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_057 on pfs_months differs across racial groups: certain races show steeper PFS decrement per feature_057 step than others.

analyses.result_summary: Partial F-test for feature_057 x race interaction: F(8, 49981) = 0.67, p = 0.720. No detectable racial heterogeneity in the feature_057 prognostic gradient. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_057 on pfs_months differs by insurance: uninsured/medicaid patients show steeper PFS decrement at worse feature_057 levels than private/medicare patients.

analyses.result_summary: Partial F-test for feature_057 x insurance interaction: F(6, 49984) = 2.03, p = 0.0585. Borderline; does not pass conventional alpha=0.05 cutoff. Hypothesis weakly suggested but not statistically supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the worst-prognosis stratum (feature_057==2), there are still no racial differences in pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[sub.loc[sub['feature_018']==r,'pfs_months'] for r in races]) on subset feature_057==2
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_057==2 (n=7437): mean pfs_months by race - asian 2.436, black 2.311, hispanic 2.325, other 2.463, white 2.393. ANOVA F = 0.895, p = 0.466. No detectable racial differences within this high-risk subgroup. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_078 and pfs_months is nonlinear (convex/quadratic): a quadratic feature_078 term improves model fit substantially over linear-only.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_078**2 + ...', data=df).fit() vs linear model
analyses.result_summary: Adding feature_078^2: F = 5604.6, p ~ 0. Quadratic coefficient = +0.000834 (positive convex curvature). The age-PFS relationship accelerates at higher feature_078 values. Hypothesis strongly supported; the linear-only model materially mis-specifies the dose-response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full multivariable model (feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_feature_013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_051) explains nearly all variance in pfs_months in this cohort, indicating pfs_months is essentially a deterministic function of measured prognostic features.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_feature_013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_051', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: R^2 = 0.9699, Adjusted R^2 = 0.9699 with n=50000. All non-feature_051 coefficients have |t| > 28 and p ~ 0. Residual SSR is tiny relative to total. Confirms an extremely high-signal cohort where the listed features capture nearly all PFS variation. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among binary features, feature_115 and feature_104 show borderline univariate signals (p~0.06 and 0.07) and these signals do NOT survive adjustment for the multivariable prognostic block, consistent with chance/marginal confounding rather than true independent effects.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_115 + adjusters'); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_104 + adjusters')
analyses.result_summary: feature_115 adjusted coef = +0.0025 (p = 0.54). feature_104 adjusted coef = +0.0069 (p = 0.12). Both null after adjustment, supporting the hypothesis that these borderline univariate signals are not independent predictors.
